title: Mark 1
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Mark
paragraphs: 1 This is the beginning of the gospel about Jesus Christ, the Son of God.
2 The prophet Isaiah wrote about him long ago. God said: "I will send my messenger ahead of you. He will prepare your way."
3 "A voice will call out in the desert: 'Prepare the way for the Lord. Make straight paths for him.'"
4 So John the Baptist came to the desert. He preached that people should turn from their sins and be baptized. Then God would forgive them.
5 People came to him from all over Jerusalem and Judea. They confessed their sins. John baptized them in the Jordan River.
6 John wore clothes made of camel hair. He had a leather belt around his waist. He ate locusts and wild honey.
7 He announced, "Someone more powerful than I am will come after me. I am not even good enough to bend down and untie his sandals."
8 "I baptize you with water. But he will baptize you with the Holy Spirit."
9 In those days, Jesus came from Nazareth in Galilee. John baptized him in the Jordan River.
10 As Jesus came up out of the water, he saw heaven open. The Spirit came down on him like a dove.
11 A voice from heaven said, "You are my dear Son. I am very pleased with you."
12 At once, the Spirit led Jesus into the desert.
13 He stayed there for forty days. Satan tested him there. He was with the wild animals. And the angels took care of him.
14 After John was put in prison, Jesus went into Galilee. He preached the gospel of God.
15 He said, "The time has come. The kingdom of God is near. Turn from your sins and believe the good news!"
16 Jesus was walking by the Sea of Galilee. He saw Simon and his brother Andrew. They were throwing a net into the water. They were fishermen.
17 "Come, follow me," Jesus said, "and I will make you fishers of people."
18 Right away, they left their nets and followed him.
19 A little farther on, Jesus saw two more brothers — James and John, the sons of Zebedee. They were in a boat, fixing their nets.
20 Right away Jesus called them. They left their father Zebedee in the boat with the hired workers. Then they followed Jesus.
21 Jesus and his friends went to Capernaum. On the Sabbath, Jesus went into the synagogue and began to teach.
22 The people were amazed at his teaching. He taught with real authority, not like the scribes.
23 Suddenly, a man with an evil spirit cried out in the synagogue.
24 "What do you want with us, Jesus of Nazareth?" he shouted. "Have you come to destroy us? I know who you are — you are the Holy One of God!"
25 But Jesus commanded the evil spirit, "Be quiet! Come out of him!"
26 The evil spirit shook the man hard. Then it came out of him with a loud scream.
27 Everyone was amazed. They asked each other, "What is this? A new teaching, and with authority! He even gives orders to evil spirits, and they obey him!"
28 News about Jesus spread quickly through the whole area of Galilee.
29 Jesus and his friends left the synagogue. They went to the home of Simon and Andrew, with James and John.
30 Simon's mother-in-law was sick in bed with a fever. They told Jesus about her right away.
31 Jesus went over to her, took her by the hand, and helped her up. The fever left her. Then she began to serve them.
32 That evening, after sunset, people brought all the sick to Jesus. They also brought those who had demons in them.
33 The whole town gathered at the door.
34 Jesus healed many who had different diseases. He also drove out many demons. But he would not let the demons speak, because they knew who he was.
35 Very early in the morning, while it was still dark, Jesus got up. He left the house and went to a quiet place to pray.
36 Simon and his friends went out to look for him.
37 When they found him, they said, "Everyone is looking for you!"
38 Jesus answered, "Let's go somewhere else — to the nearby villages. I need to preach there too. That is why I came."
39 So he traveled all over Galilee. He preached in their synagogues and drove out demons.
40 A man with leprosy came to Jesus. He fell on his knees and begged him. "If you are willing," he said, "you can make me clean."
41 Jesus felt sorry for him. He reached out his hand and touched the man. "I am willing," he said. "Be clean!"
42 At once, the leprosy left him, and the man was clean.
43 Jesus sent him away with a strong warning.
44 "Don't tell anyone about this," he said. "But go and show yourself to the priest. Bring the offering Moses commanded, as a proof to them."

45 But the man went out and started to spread the news. He told everyone what had happened. So Jesus could no longer enter a town in public. He stayed outside in quiet places. Yet people still came to him from everywhere.

title: Mark 2
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Mark
paragraphs: 1 A few days later, Jesus went back to Capernaum. The people heard that he was home.
2 So many came that there was no room left — not even outside the door. Jesus was preaching the word to them.
3 Then some men brought a paralyzed man to Jesus. Four of them were carrying him.
4 The crowd was so thick they could not get close to Jesus. So they made a hole in the roof above him. Then they lowered the paralyzed man down on his mat.
5 When Jesus saw their faith, he said to the paralyzed man, "Son, your sins are forgiven."
6 But some of the scribes were sitting there. They were thinking in their hearts,
7 "Why does this man talk like this? He is blaspheming! Only God can forgive sins."
8 At once Jesus knew in his spirit what they were thinking. He asked them, "Why are you thinking these things in your hearts?
9 Which is easier — to say to this man, 'Your sins are forgiven,' or to say, 'Get up, pick up your mat, and walk'?
10 But I will show you that the Son of Man has the right to forgive sins on earth." Then he said to the paralyzed man,
11 "I tell you, get up. Pick up your mat and go home."
12 Right away, the man got up. He picked up his mat and walked out in front of them all. Everyone was amazed. They praised God and said, "We have never seen anything like this!"
13 Jesus went out beside the sea again. A crowd of people came to him, and he taught them there.
14 As he was walking, Jesus saw a man named Levi, the son of Alphaeus. He was sitting at the tax booth. Jesus said to him, "Follow me." Levi got up and followed him.
15 Later, Jesus was eating at Levi's house. Many tax collectors and sinners were eating with him and his disciples. Many of these people were following Jesus.
16 The scribes who were Pharisees saw Jesus eating with these people. They asked his disciples, "Why does he eat with tax collectors and sinners?"
17 Jesus heard this and answered, "Healthy people don't need a doctor. Sick people do. I did not come to call righteous people, but sinners."
18 John's disciples and the Pharisees often fasted. So some people came to Jesus and asked, "John's disciples fast. The Pharisees' disciples fast. Why don't your disciples fast?"
19 Jesus answered, "How can the guests at a wedding fast while the bridegroom is with them? As long as he is with them, they cannot fast.
20 But the time will come when the bridegroom is taken from them. Then they will fast.
21 No one sews a patch of new cloth on an old coat. If he does, the new patch will pull away from the old coat. And the tear will be worse than before.
22 And no one pours new wine into old wineskins. If he does, the wine will burst the skins. Both the wine and the wineskins will be ruined. New wine must be poured into new wineskins."
23 One Sabbath, Jesus was walking through some grainfields. His disciples began to pick the heads of grain as they walked.
24 The Pharisees said to Jesus, "Look! Why are they doing what is not allowed on the Sabbath?"
25 Jesus answered, "Have you never read what David did? He and his men were hungry and needed food.
26 In the time of Abiathar the high priest, David went into the house of God. He ate the holy bread that only the priests were allowed to eat. He even gave some to his men."
27 Then Jesus said, "The Sabbath was made for man. Man was not made for the Sabbath.
28 So the Son of Man is Lord even of the Sabbath."

title: Mark 3
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Mark
paragraphs: 1 Jesus went into the synagogue again. A man with a crippled hand was there.
2 Some people were watching Jesus closely. They wanted to accuse him. So they waited to see if he would heal on the Sabbath.
3 Jesus said to the man with the crippled hand, "Stand up here in front of everyone."
4 Then Jesus asked the people, "Which is right on the Sabbath — to do good or to do evil? To save a life or to destroy it?" But they said nothing.
5 Jesus looked around at them. He was angry and sad because their hearts were hard. Then he said to the man, "Stretch out your hand." The man stretched it out, and his hand was healed.
6 The Pharisees went out at once. They began to plan with the Herodians how to kill Jesus.
7 So Jesus went away with his disciples to the sea. A large crowd from Galilee followed him. Many also came from Judea,
8 from Jerusalem, from Idumea, from the lands east of the Jordan, and from the area around Tyre and Sidon. The crowd was huge. They had heard about all the great things Jesus was doing.
9 Jesus told his disciples to keep a small boat ready for him. The crowd was so big, he didn't want them to crush him.
10 He had healed many people. Now all the sick were pushing forward to touch him.
11 Whenever the evil spirits saw Jesus, they fell down before him. They cried out, "You are the Son of God!"
12 But Jesus warned them strongly not to tell people who he was.
13 Then Jesus went up on a mountain. He called the people he wanted, and they came to him.
14 He chose twelve men. He called them apostles. They were to be with him and to be sent out to preach.
15 He also gave them power to drive out demons.
16 These are the twelve he chose: Simon (whom Jesus named Peter),
17 James son of Zebedee and his brother John (whom Jesus named Boanerges, which means "Sons of Thunder"),
18 Andrew, Philip, Bartholomew, Matthew, Thomas, James son of Alphaeus, Thaddaeus, Simon the Zealot,
19 and Judas Iscariot, who later betrayed Jesus.
20 Then Jesus went home. A crowd gathered again, so that he and his disciples could not even eat.
21 When his family heard about this, they went out to take him home. They were saying, "He has lost his mind."
22 The scribes who had come down from Jerusalem were saying, "He has Beelzebul in him! He drives out demons by the power of the prince of demons."
23 So Jesus called them together. He began to speak to them in parables. He said, "How can Satan drive out Satan?
24 If a kingdom fights against itself, it cannot stand.
25 If a family fights against itself, it cannot stand.
26 And if Satan fights against himself, he cannot stand. His end has come.
27 No one can go into a strong man's house and steal his things — unless he first ties up the strong man. Then he can take everything in his house.
28 I tell you the truth: people will be forgiven for every sin and every blasphemy they speak.
29 But whoever blasphemes against the Holy Spirit will never be forgiven. That person is guilty of a sin that lasts forever."
30 Jesus said this because they were saying, "He has an evil spirit in him."
31 Then Jesus' mother and brothers came. They stood outside and sent someone in to call him.
32 A crowd was sitting around Jesus. They told him, "Look, your mother and brothers are outside. They are asking for you."
33 But Jesus replied, "Who is my mother? Who are my brothers?"
34 He looked at the people sitting around him in a circle and said, "Here are my mother and my brothers!
35 Anyone who does what God wants is my brother and sister and mother."

title: Mark 4
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Mark
paragraphs: 1 Jesus began to teach beside the sea again. Such a large crowd gathered around him that he got into a boat. He sat in the boat, while all the people stood along the shore.
2 He taught them many things in parables. As he was teaching, he said,
3 "Listen! A farmer went out to plant seeds.
4 As he was scattering the seeds, some fell on the path. The birds came and ate them up.
5 Some seeds fell on rocky ground, where there was little soil. They grew up quickly because the soil was shallow.
6 But when the sun came up, the young plants were burned. They dried up because they had no roots.
7 Other seeds fell among thorns. The thorns grew up and choked the young plants, so they did not produce any fruit.
8 But some seeds fell on good soil. They grew up and produced a crop — some 30 times more, some 60 times more, and some 100 times more."
9 Then Jesus said, "Anyone who has ears, let him listen!"
10 When Jesus was alone with the Twelve and the others around him, they asked him about the parable.
11 He told them, "The secret of the kingdom of God has been given to you. But to those outside, everything is taught in parables.
12 So that, 'they may always see but never understand. They may always hear but never know. If they did, they might turn back and be forgiven.'"
13 Then Jesus said to them, "Don't you understand this parable? How will you understand any of the parables?
14 The farmer plants the word.
15 Some people are like the seeds on the path. When they hear the word, Satan comes right away and takes the word out of them.
16 Others are like the seeds on rocky ground. When they hear the word, they receive it with joy at once.
17 But they have no root, so they do not last. When trouble comes, or when people attack them because of the word, they quickly turn away.
18 Others are like the seeds sown among the thorns. They hear the word,
19 but the worries of this life, the false promises of money, and the desire for other things come in. These choke the word, and it does not produce fruit.
20 Others are like the seeds on good soil. They hear the word, receive it, and produce a crop — 30 times more, 60 times more, or 100 times more."
21 Jesus also said, "Does anyone bring in a lamp to put it under a basket or under a bed? No — he puts it on a stand.
22 Nothing is hidden that will not be shown. Nothing is kept secret that will not come to light.
23 If anyone has ears, let him listen!"
24 He went on to say, "Be careful about what you hear. The way you measure others is the way you will be measured. And even more will be added to you.
25 Whoever has will be given more. But whoever does not have — even what he has will be taken away."
26 Jesus also said, "The kingdom of God is like a man who scatters seed on the ground.
27 He sleeps at night and wakes up in the day. The seed grows and sprouts up, but he doesn't know how it happens.
28 The earth produces a crop by itself — first the stalk, then the head, and then the full grain.
29 As soon as the grain is ripe, he cuts it with a sickle, because the harvest has come."
30 Then Jesus asked, "What is the kingdom of God like? What story can we use to describe it?
31 It is like a mustard seed. When it is planted, it is the smallest seed on earth.
32 But after it is planted, it grows up. It becomes the largest of all garden plants. It has such big branches that birds can rest in its shade."
33 Jesus told them many parables like these. He spoke the word in a way they could understand.
34 He always used parables when he spoke to the crowd. But when he was alone with his disciples, he explained everything to them.
35 That evening, Jesus said to his disciples, "Let's cross to the other side of the lake."
36 So they left the crowd and took Jesus with them in the boat. He was already in it. Other boats were with him too.
37 Soon a strong storm came up. The waves crashed over the boat, and it was filling with water.
38 Jesus was in the back of the boat, sleeping on a pillow. The disciples woke him up. "Teacher!" they said. "Don't you care that we are about to die?"
39 Jesus got up. He ordered the wind and the sea, "Quiet! Be still!" The wind died down, and everything was totally calm.
40 He said to them, "Why are you so afraid? Don't you have any faith yet?"
41 They were very afraid. They asked each other, "Who is this man? Even the wind and the sea obey him!"

title: Mark 5
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Mark
paragraphs: 1 They came to the other side of the lake, to the region of the Gerasenes.
2 As soon as Jesus got out of the boat, a man came toward him. The man had an evil spirit in him and was coming from the tombs.
3 He lived in the tombs. No one could hold him down, not even with chains.
4 He had been tied up with chains and leg-irons many times. But he had broken the chains and the leg-irons. No one was strong enough to control him.
5 Night and day, in the tombs and on the hills, he kept screaming and cutting himself with stones.
6 When he saw Jesus from far away, he ran to him and fell on his knees.
7 He shouted in a loud voice, "What do you want with me, Jesus, Son of the Most High God? In God's name, please don't hurt me!"
8 Jesus had already said to him, "Come out of this man, you evil spirit!"
9 "What is your name?" Jesus asked. "My name is Legion," he answered, "because we are many."
10 He begged Jesus again and again not to send them out of that area.
11 A large herd of pigs was eating on a nearby hill.
12 The demons begged Jesus, "Send us into the pigs. Let us go into them."
13 Jesus let them. The evil spirits came out of the man and went into the pigs. The herd of about 2,000 pigs rushed down the steep slope into the lake and drowned.
14 The men who were watching the pigs ran away. They told what had happened in the town and in the countryside. The people came out to see what had happened.
15 When they came to Jesus, they saw the man who had been controlled by the legion of demons. He was sitting there with clothes on and thinking clearly. They were afraid.
16 Those who had seen it told the people what had happened to the man and to the pigs.
17 The people began to beg Jesus to leave their area.
18 As Jesus was getting into the boat, the man who had been controlled by demons begged to go with him.
19 But Jesus did not let him. "Go home to your family," Jesus said. "Tell them how much the Lord has done for you. Tell them how he has shown you mercy."
20 So the man went away. He began to tell everyone in the Decapolis how much Jesus had done for him. And everyone was amazed.
21 Jesus crossed back over to the other side of the lake. A large crowd gathered around him beside the sea.
22 A synagogue leader named Jairus came up. When he saw Jesus, he fell down at his feet.
23 He begged Jesus with strong feelings, "My little daughter is dying. Please come and put your hands on her, so she will get well and live."
24 So Jesus went with him. A large crowd followed and pressed in around him.
25 A woman was there who had been bleeding for twelve years.
26 She had suffered a lot from many doctors. She had spent everything she had, but nothing worked. In fact, she had become worse.
27 The woman heard about Jesus. She came up behind him through the crowd and touched his coat.
28 She kept saying to herself, "If I just touch his clothes, I will be healed."
29 Right away, her bleeding stopped. She felt in her body that she was healed of her sickness.
30 At once, Jesus knew that power had gone out from him. He turned around in the crowd and asked, "Who touched my clothes?"
31 His disciples answered, "You can see the crowd pressing in around you. And you ask, 'Who touched me?'"
32 But Jesus kept looking around to see who had done it.
33 Then the woman, knowing what had happened to her, came forward. She fell down before Jesus, shaking with fear. She told him everything.
34 Jesus said to her, "Daughter, your faith has healed you. Go in peace. Be free from your sickness."
35 While Jesus was still speaking, some men came from the house of Jairus. They said, "Your daughter is dead. Why bother the Teacher any more?"
36 But Jesus overheard what they said. He told Jairus, "Don't be afraid. Just believe."
37 Jesus did not let anyone go with him except Peter, James, and John (the brother of James).
38 They came to the house of the synagogue leader. Jesus saw a lot of noise — people were crying and wailing loudly.
39 He went inside and asked, "Why are you making such a noise? Why are you crying? The child is not dead. She is only sleeping."
40 They laughed at him. But Jesus sent everyone out of the house. He took the child's father and mother and his own companions, and went in to see the child.
41 He took her by the hand and said, "Talitha koum!" This means, "Little girl, I tell you, get up!"
42 Right away, the girl got up and began to walk around. She was twelve years old. The people were completely amazed.
43 Then Jesus told them strongly that no one should know about this. He also told them to give her something to eat.

title: Mark 6
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Mark
paragraphs: 1 Jesus left there and came to his hometown. His disciples went with him.

2 When the Sabbath came, Jesus began to teach in the synagogue. Many who heard him were amazed. They asked, "Where did this man get these ideas? What is this wisdom he has been given? And how can he do such miracles?
3 Isn't this the carpenter, the son of Mary? Isn't he the brother of James, Joses, Judas, and Simon? Aren't his sisters here with us too?" And they refused to accept him.
4 Then Jesus said to them, "A prophet has honor everywhere — except in his own hometown, with his own family, and in his own house."
5 So Jesus could not do any miracles there. He only put his hands on a few sick people and healed them.
6 He was amazed at their lack of faith. Then Jesus went around from village to village, teaching the people.
7 Jesus called the Twelve to him. He began to send them out two by two. He gave them power over evil spirits.
8 He told them to take nothing for the trip except a walking stick. No bread, no bag, no money in their belts.
9 They could wear sandals, but not a second shirt.
10 He said to them, "When you enter a house, stay there until you leave that area.
11 If anyone will not welcome you or listen to you, leave that place. Shake the dust off your feet as a warning to them."
12 So they went out and preached that people should repent.
13 They also drove out many demons. They healed many sick people, putting oil on them.
14 King Herod heard about Jesus, because his name had become well known. People were saying, "John the Baptist has risen from the dead! That is why miraculous powers are at work in him."
15 Others said, "He is Elijah." Still others said, "He is a prophet, like one of the old prophets."
16 But when Herod heard this, he said, "John, whom I beheaded, has risen from the dead!"
17 Herod himself had ordered John to be arrested. He had tied him up and put him in prison. He did this because of Herodias, his brother Philip's wife. Herod had married her.
18 John had been telling Herod, "It is not right for you to have your brother's wife!"
19 So Herodias hated John and wanted to kill him. But she had not been able to,
20 because Herod was afraid of John and protected him. Herod knew that John was a righteous and holy man. When he listened to John, he was very confused. Yet he liked to hear him.
21 Then the right time came for Herodias. On Herod's birthday, Herod held a big dinner for his nobles, his army leaders, and the leading men of Galilee.
22 The daughter of Herodias came in and danced. She pleased Herod and his guests. So the king said to the girl, "Ask me for anything you want, and I will give it to you."
23 He promised her, "Whatever you ask for, I will give you — up to half of my kingdom!"
24 The girl went out and asked her mother, "What should I ask for?" Her mother answered, "The head of John the Baptist."
25 At once, the girl hurried back to the king with her request. "I want you to give me the head of John the Baptist on a plate — right now."
26 The king was very sad. But because of his promises and his guests, he did not want to say no to her.
27 So he sent a soldier right away. The soldier was ordered to bring back John's head. He went and cut off John's head in the prison.
28 He brought the head on a plate and gave it to the girl. The girl gave it to her mother.
29 When John's disciples heard about this, they came and took John's body. They placed it in a tomb.
30 The apostles came back to Jesus. They told him about everything they had done and taught.
31 Jesus said to them, "Come with me to a quiet place by yourselves. Let's rest for a while." Many people were coming and going, so they did not even have time to eat.
32 So they went away by themselves in a boat to a quiet place.
33 But many people saw them leaving and recognized them. They ran together on foot from all the towns. They got there before Jesus and his disciples.
34 When Jesus stepped out of the boat, he saw a large crowd. He felt sorry for them, because they were like sheep without a shepherd. So he began to teach them many things.
35 By now it was getting late. The disciples came to Jesus and said, "This place is empty, and the day is almost over.
36 Send the crowd away. Then they can go to the nearby countryside and villages and buy themselves something to eat."
37 But Jesus told them, "You give them something to eat." They asked him, "Should we go and spend 200 silver coins to give all of them bread to eat?"
38 "Go and see how many loaves of bread you have," he told them. After they checked, they said, "Five loaves — and two fish."
39 Then Jesus told them to have the people sit in groups on the green grass.
40 So they sat down in groups of one hundred and groups of fifty.

41 Jesus took the five loaves and the two fish. He looked up to heaven, said a blessing, and broke the loaves. Then he gave the bread to his disciples to set in front of the people. He also divided the two fish among them all.
42 They all ate and were full.
43 The disciples picked up twelve baskets of leftover bread and fish.
44 The number of men who had eaten the bread was 5,000.
45 Right away, Jesus made his disciples get into the boat and go on ahead of him to Bethsaida. He stayed to send the crowd away.
46 After he said goodbye to them, he went up on the mountain to pray.
47 When evening came, the boat was in the middle of the sea. Jesus was alone on land.

48 He could see that the disciples were struggling to row, because the wind was blowing against them. Very early in the morning, Jesus came out to them, walking on the sea. He was about to walk past them.
49 But when they saw him walking on the sea, they cried out. They thought he was a ghost.
50 They all saw him and were terrified. But Jesus spoke to them right away. "Be brave!" he said. "It is me. Don't be afraid."
51 Then he climbed into the boat with them. The wind died down. The disciples were completely amazed.
52 They had not understood about the loaves. Their hearts were closed.
53 When they had crossed over, they came to land at Gennesaret and tied up the boat.
54 As soon as they got out of the boat, the people recognized Jesus.
55 They ran through the whole region. They began to carry the sick on mats to wherever they heard Jesus was.

56 Wherever he went — in villages, towns, or countrysides — they laid the sick in the marketplaces. They begged him to let them just touch the edge of his coat. And everyone who touched him was healed.

title: Mark 7
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Mark
paragraphs: 1 The Pharisees and some scribes came from Jerusalem and gathered around Jesus.
2 They saw that some of his disciples were eating with unclean hands. That is, they had not washed.
3 The Pharisees and all the Jews follow the tradition of the elders. They do not eat until they wash their hands in the proper way.
4 When they come back from the market, they do not eat unless they wash. They have many other traditions they follow. These include washing cups, pitchers, pots, and dining couches.
5 So the Pharisees and scribes asked Jesus, "Why don't your disciples follow the tradition of the elders? Instead, they eat with unclean hands."
6 Jesus answered them, "Isaiah was right when he spoke about you hypocrites. As it is written: 'These people honor me with their lips, but their hearts are far from me.
7 They worship me for nothing. They teach the rules of men as if they were God's teachings.'
8 You have ignored God's command to keep the traditions of men."
9 He went on to say, "You cleverly set aside God's command to keep your own traditions.
10 For Moses said, 'Honor your father and your mother,' and, 'Anyone who curses his father or mother must be put to death.'
11 But you say that if a man tells his father or mother, 'Whatever I could have given to help you is Corban' — that means a gift given to God —
12 then he is no longer allowed to do anything for his father or mother.
13 So you cancel the word of God by your tradition, which you have passed down. And you do many other things like this."
14 Jesus called the crowd to him again. He said, "All of you, listen to me and understand:
15 Nothing that enters a person from the outside can make him unclean. But the things that come out of a person — these are what make him unclean."
16 [Some early manuscripts add: "If anyone has ears to hear, let him hear."]
17 When Jesus left the crowd and went into the house, his disciples asked him about the parable.
18 "Are you still so slow to understand?" he asked. "Don't you see? Nothing that enters a person from the outside can make him unclean.
19 It doesn't go into his heart. It goes into his stomach and then leaves the body." (By saying this, Jesus made all foods clean.)
20 He continued, "What comes out of a person — that is what makes him unclean.
21 From inside the human heart come evil thoughts, sexual sins, stealing, murder, adultery,
22 greed, evil, lying, wild living, envy, saying bad things about people, pride, and foolishness.
23 All these evils come from inside. These are what make a person unclean."
24 Jesus left that place and went to the region of Tyre. He did not want anyone to know he was there. He went into a house, but he could not stay hidden.
25 A woman whose little daughter had an evil spirit heard about him. She came at once and fell at his feet.
26 The woman was Greek, born in Syrophoenicia. She kept asking Jesus to make the demon leave her daughter.
27 "First let the children eat all they want," Jesus said. "It is not right to take the children's bread and throw it to the dogs."
28 "Yes, Lord," she replied. "But even the dogs under the table eat the children's crumbs."
29 Then Jesus told her, "Because of this answer, you may go. The demon has left your daughter."
30 She went home and found her child lying on the bed. The demon was gone.
31 Jesus left the region of Tyre. He went through Sidon to the Sea of Galilee. He came into the region of the Decapolis.
32 Some people brought to him a man who was deaf and could barely speak. They begged Jesus to put his hand on the man.
33 Jesus took the man aside, away from the crowd. He put his fingers into the man's ears. Then he spit and touched the man's tongue.
34 He looked up to heaven and gave a deep sigh. He said to the man, "Ephphatha!" — which means, "Be opened!"
35 Right away, the man's ears were opened. His tongue was set free, and he began to speak clearly.
36 Jesus ordered them not to tell anyone. But the more he ordered them, the more they spread the news.
37 The people were completely amazed. They said, "He has done all things well! He even makes deaf people hear and people who could not speak now speak!"

title: Mark 8
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Mark
paragraphs: 1 In those days, the crowd became very large again, and they had nothing to eat. Jesus called the disciples to him and said,
2 "I feel sorry for this crowd. They have been with me for three days and have nothing to eat.
3 If I send them home hungry, they will faint along the way. Some of them have come a long distance."
4 His disciples answered, "Where in this empty place could anyone find enough bread to feed all these people?"
5 "How many loaves do you have?" Jesus asked. "Seven," they replied.

6 He told the crowd to sit down on the ground. Then he took the seven loaves, gave thanks, and broke them. He gave the bread to his disciples to set in front of the people. The disciples passed it out to the crowd.
7 They also had a few small fish. Jesus blessed them and told the disciples to set these in front of the people too.
8 The people ate and were full. The disciples picked up seven baskets of leftover pieces.
9 About 4,000 men were there. After Jesus had sent the crowd away,
10 he got into the boat with his disciples and went to the area of Dalmanutha.
11 The Pharisees came and began to argue with Jesus. They tested him by asking for a sign from heaven.
12 Jesus sighed deeply in his spirit and said, "Why does this generation ask for a sign? I tell you the truth: no sign will be given to this generation."
13 He left them, got back into the boat, and crossed to the other side.
14 The disciples had forgotten to take bread. They had only one loaf with them in the boat.
15 "Watch out!" Jesus warned them. "Be careful of the yeast of the Pharisees and of Herod."
16 They began to talk among themselves about the fact that they had no bread.
17 Jesus knew what they were saying. He asked them, "Why are you talking about having no bread? Don't you see or understand yet? Are your hearts so closed?
18 'You have eyes — can't you see? You have ears — can't you hear?' Don't you remember?
19 When I broke the five loaves for the 5,000, how many baskets of leftover pieces did you collect?" "Twelve," they answered.
20 "And when I broke the seven loaves for the 4,000, how many baskets of leftover pieces did you collect?" "Seven," they said.
21 Then he asked them, "Do you still not understand?"
22 When they came to Bethsaida, some people brought a blind man to Jesus. They begged him to touch the man.
23 Jesus took the blind man by the hand and led him out of the village. He spit on the man's eyes and put his hands on him. "Can you see anything?" he asked.
24 The man looked up and said, "I can see the people, but they look like trees walking around."
25 Once more, Jesus put his hands on the man's eyes. When the man opened his eyes, his sight came back. He could see everything clearly.
26 Jesus sent him home and said, "Don't go back into the village."
27 Then Jesus and his disciples went on to the villages near Caesarea Philippi. On the way, Jesus asked his disciples, "Who do people say I am?"
28 They answered, "Some say John the Baptist. Others say Elijah. Still others say one of the prophets."
29 "But what about you?" Jesus asked. "Who do you say I am?" Peter answered, "You are the Christ."
30 Jesus warned them not to tell anyone about him.
31 Then he began to teach them that the Son of Man must suffer many things. He must be rejected by the elders, the chief priests, and the scribes. He must be killed, and after three days, rise again.
32 He said this openly. Peter took him aside and began to rebuke him.
33 But Jesus turned around and looked at his disciples. He rebuked Peter and said, "Get behind me, Satan! You are not thinking the things of God, but the things of men."
34 Then Jesus called the crowd to him along with his disciples. He told them, "If anyone wants to follow me, he must deny himself, take up his cross, and follow me.
35 Whoever wants to save his life will lose it. But whoever loses his life for me and for the gospel will save it.
36 What good is it for a person to gain the whole world but lose his soul?
37 What can a person give in exchange for his soul?

38 If anyone is ashamed of me and my words in this generation — which is unfaithful and full of sin — the Son of Man will also be ashamed of him when he comes in his Father's glory with the holy angels."

title: Mark 9
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Mark
paragraphs: 1 Then Jesus said to them, "I tell you the truth: some of you standing here will not die before you see the kingdom of God come with power."
2 Six days later, Jesus took Peter, James, and John, and led them up a high mountain alone. There, his appearance was changed in front of them.
3 His clothes became shining white — whiter than anyone who washes clothes on earth could make them.
4 Then Elijah and Moses appeared before them. They were talking with Jesus.
5 Peter said to Jesus, "Rabbi, it is good for us to be here. Let us put up three shelters — one for you, one for Moses, and one for Elijah."
6 He said this because they were all so afraid. He did not know what else to say.
7 Then a cloud appeared and covered them. A voice came from the cloud: "This is my beloved Son. Listen to him!"
8 Suddenly, when they looked around, they saw no one but Jesus.
9 As they were coming down the mountain, Jesus ordered them not to tell anyone what they had seen. They could only tell people after the Son of Man had risen from the dead.
10 So they kept the matter to themselves. They talked together about what "rise from the dead" might mean.
11 They asked Jesus, "Why do the scribes say that Elijah must come first?"
12 He replied, "Elijah does come first, and he restores all things. But why is it written that the Son of Man must suffer many things and be rejected?
13 I tell you that Elijah has already come. People did to him whatever they wanted, just as it is written about him."
14 When they came back to the other disciples, they saw a large crowd around them. The scribes were arguing with them.
15 As soon as the people saw Jesus, they were filled with awe and ran to greet him.
16 "What are you arguing about with them?" he asked.
17 Someone in the crowd answered, "Teacher, I brought you my son. He has a spirit that makes him unable to speak.
18 Whenever it takes hold of him, it throws him to the ground. He foams at the mouth, grinds his teeth, and becomes stiff. I asked your disciples to make it leave, but they could not."
19 Jesus replied, "You unbelieving generation! How long must I stay with you? How long must I put up with you? Bring the boy to me."
20 So they brought him. As soon as the spirit saw Jesus, it threw the boy into a fit. He fell to the ground and rolled around, foaming at the mouth.
21 Jesus asked the boy's father, "How long has this been happening to him?" "From when he was a child," he said.
22 "Often the spirit throws him into the fire or into the water to kill him. But if you can do anything, please feel sorry for us and help us."
23 "If you can?" Jesus said. "All things are possible for the one who believes!"
24 Right away, the boy's father cried out, "I do believe — help me with my unbelief!"
25 When Jesus saw that a crowd was running up, he rebuked the evil spirit. "You deaf and mute spirit," he said, "I command you to come out of him and never enter him again."
26 The spirit screamed and shook the boy hard, and then it came out. The boy was like a dead body, so that many said, "He is dead."
27 But Jesus took him by the hand and helped him up, and he stood.
28 After Jesus had gone into the house, his disciples asked him in private, "Why couldn't we make it leave?"
29 Jesus answered, "This kind can come out only by prayer."
30 They left there and passed through Galilee. Jesus did not want anyone to know,
31 because he was teaching his disciples. He told them, "The Son of Man will be handed over to men. They will kill him. After three days, he will rise again."
32 But they did not understand what he said. And they were afraid to ask him about it.
33 They came to Capernaum. While Jesus was in the house, he asked them, "What were you talking about on the way?"
34 But they were silent. On the way, they had been arguing about who was the greatest.
35 Jesus sat down and called the Twelve. He said, "If anyone wants to be first, he must be the last of all and the servant of all."
36 Then he had a little child stand in front of them. He took the child in his arms and said to them,
37 "Whoever welcomes one of these little children in my name welcomes me. And whoever welcomes me does not only welcome me, but also the One who sent me."
38 John said to Jesus, "Teacher, we saw someone else driving out demons in your name. We tried to stop him, because he is not one of us."
39 "Do not stop him," Jesus replied. "No one who does a miracle in my name can quickly turn around and say bad things about me.
40 Whoever is not against us is for us.
41 I tell you the truth: if anyone gives you even a cup of water because you belong to Christ, he will not lose his reward.
42 But if anyone makes one of these little ones who believe in me fall into sin, it would be better for him to have a large millstone hung around his neck and to be thrown into the sea.
43 If your hand makes you sin, cut it off. It is better to enter life crippled than to have two hands and go into hell, into the fire that never goes out.
44 [Some manuscripts add: "where 'their worm never dies, and the fire is never quenched.'"]
45 If your foot makes you sin, cut it off. It is better to enter life unable to walk than to have two feet and be thrown into hell.
46 [Some manuscripts add: "where 'their worm never dies, and the fire is never quenched.'"]
47 If your eye makes you sin, tear it out. It is better to enter the kingdom of God with one eye than to have two eyes and be thrown into hell,
48 where 'their worm never dies, and the fire is never put out.'
49 Everyone will be tested by fire — as if they were salted with fire.
50 Salt is good. But if salt loses its salt taste, how can you make it salty again? Have salt in yourselves, and be at peace with one another."

title: Mark 10
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Mark
paragraphs: 1 Then Jesus left that place and went into the area of Judea, east of the Jordan. The crowds came to him again, and he taught them, as he usually did.
2 Some Pharisees came to test him. They asked, "Is it allowed by the law for a man to divorce his wife?"
3 "What did Moses command you?" he replied.
4 They answered, "Moses allowed a man to write his wife a certificate of divorce and send her away."
5 But Jesus told them, "Moses wrote this command because of your hard hearts.
6 But from the beginning of creation, 'God made them male and female.'
7 'For this reason, a man will leave his father and mother and be joined to his wife,
8 and the two will become one flesh.' So they are no longer two, but one flesh.
9 Therefore what God has joined together, let no one separate."
10 When they were back inside the house, the disciples asked Jesus about this matter.
11 So he told them, "Whoever divorces his wife and marries another woman commits adultery against her.
12 And if a woman divorces her husband and marries another man, she commits adultery."
13 People were bringing little children to Jesus so that he could put his hands on them. But the disciples scolded those who brought them.
14 When Jesus saw this, he was angry. He told them, "Let the little children come to me. Don't stop them! The kingdom of God belongs to people like these.
15 I tell you the truth: anyone who does not receive the kingdom of God like a little child will never enter it."
16 Then he took the children in his arms, put his hands on them, and blessed them.
17 As Jesus started on his way, a man ran up to him and knelt before him. "Good Teacher," he asked, "what must I do to inherit eternal life?"
18 "Why do you call me good?" Jesus replied. "No one is good except God alone.
19 You know the commandments: 'Do not murder. Do not commit adultery. Do not steal. Do not lie about others. Do not cheat. Honor your father and mother.'"
20 "Teacher," he replied, "I have kept all these since I was young."
21 Jesus looked at him and loved him. He said, "There is one thing you are missing. Go and sell everything you own and give to the poor. Then you will have treasure in heaven. Then come, follow me."
22 But the man was sad at these words. He went away in sorrow, because he was very rich.
23 Jesus looked around and said to his disciples, "How hard it is for the rich to enter the kingdom of God!"
24 The disciples were amazed at his words. But Jesus said to them again, "Children, how hard it is to enter the kingdom of God!
25 It is easier for a camel to go through the eye of a needle than for a rich man to enter the kingdom of God."
26 They were even more amazed. They said to each other, "Then who can be saved?"
27 Jesus looked at them and said, "With man this is impossible, but not with God. All things are possible with God."
28 Peter said to him, "Look, we have left everything and followed you."
29 "I tell you the truth," Jesus replied, "no one who has left home or brothers or sisters or mother or father or children or fields for me and for the gospel
30 will fail to receive 100 times as much in this time — houses and brothers and sisters and mothers and children and fields, along with trouble from others — and in the time to come, eternal life.
31 But many who are first will be last, and the last will be first."

32 They were going up the road to Jerusalem. Jesus was walking ahead of them. The disciples were amazed, and those who followed were afraid. Again Jesus took the Twelve aside. He began to tell them what was going to happen to him.
33 "Look, we are going up to Jerusalem. The Son of Man will be handed over to the chief priests and scribes. They will say he deserves to die. They will hand him over to the Gentiles,
34 who will make fun of him, spit on him, whip him, and kill him. And after three days he will rise again."
35 Then James and John, the sons of Zebedee, came to Jesus. "Teacher," they said, "we want you to do something for us — whatever we ask."
36 "What do you want me to do for you?" he asked.
37 They answered, "Let one of us sit at your right and the other at your left in your glory."
38 "You don't know what you are asking," Jesus replied. "Can you drink the cup I will drink? Can you go through the baptism I will go through?"
39 "We can," they answered. "You will drink the cup that I drink," Jesus said, "and you will go through the baptism that I go through.
40 But to sit at my right or left is not mine to give. These seats belong to those for whom they have been prepared."
41 When the other ten heard about this, they became angry with James and John.
42 So Jesus called them together. He said, "You know that those who are seen as rulers of the Gentiles act like rulers over them. Their leaders use their power on them.
43 But it must not be this way among you. Whoever wants to become great among you must be your servant.
44 And whoever wants to be first must be the slave of all.
45 For even the Son of Man did not come to be served, but to serve. He came to give his life as a ransom for many."
46 Then they came to Jericho. As Jesus and his disciples were leaving Jericho with a large crowd, a blind beggar was sitting beside the road. His name was Bartimaeus, son of Timaeus.
47 When he heard that it was Jesus of Nazareth, he began to cry out, "Jesus, Son of David, have mercy on me!"
48 Many people told him to be quiet. But he cried out even louder, "Son of David, have mercy on me!"
49 Jesus stopped and said, "Call him." So they called the blind man. "Be brave!" they said. "Get up! He is calling for you."
50 He threw off his coat, jumped up, and came to Jesus.
51 "What do you want me to do for you?" Jesus asked. "Rabboni," said the blind man, "let me see again."
52 "Go," said Jesus. "Your faith has healed you." Right away, the man could see. And he followed Jesus along the road.

title: Mark 11
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Mark
paragraphs: 1 As they came near Jerusalem, they reached Bethphage and Bethany at the Mount of Olives. Jesus sent out two of his disciples.
2 He said to them, "Go into the village ahead of you. As soon as you enter it, you will find a colt tied there. No one has ever sat on it. Untie it and bring it here.
3 If anyone asks, 'Why are you doing this?' tell him, 'The Lord needs it and will bring it back soon.'"
4 So they went and found the colt outside in the street, tied at a doorway. They untied it.
5 Some people standing there asked, "Why are you untying the colt?"
6 The disciples answered as Jesus had told them. And the people let them go.
7 Then they led the colt to Jesus. They threw their coats over it, and Jesus sat on it.
8 Many people in the crowd spread their coats on the road. Others spread branches they had cut from the fields.
9 Those who went ahead and those who followed were shouting: "Hosanna!" "Blessed is the one who comes in the name of the Lord!"
10 "Blessed is the coming kingdom of our father David!" "Hosanna in the highest!"
11 Then Jesus entered Jerusalem and went into the temple area. He looked around at everything. But since it was already late, he went out to Bethany with the Twelve.
12 The next day, after they left Bethany, Jesus was hungry.
13 From far away, he saw a fig tree with leaves on it. He went to see if there was any fruit on it. But when he reached it, he found nothing on it except leaves. It was not the season for figs.
14 Then he said to the tree, "May no one ever eat fruit from you again." And his disciples heard him say this.

15 When they came to Jerusalem, Jesus entered the temple area. He began to drive out those who were buying and selling there. He turned over the tables of the money changers and the seats of those who were selling doves.
16 He would not allow anyone to carry goods through the temple area.
17 Then Jesus taught them. He said, "Is it not written: 'My house will be called a house of prayer for all the nations'? But you have made it 'a hideout of robbers.'"
18 When the chief priests and scribes heard this, they looked for a way to kill him. They were afraid of him, because the whole crowd was amazed at his teaching.
19 When evening came, Jesus and his disciples went out of the city.
20 As they were walking back in the morning, they saw the fig tree. It had dried up from its roots.
21 Peter remembered and said, "Look, Rabbi! The fig tree you cursed has dried up!"
22 "Have faith in God," Jesus said to them.
23 "I tell you the truth: if anyone says to this mountain, 'Be lifted up and thrown into the sea,' and has no doubt in his heart but believes it will happen, it will be done for him.
24 So I tell you: whatever you ask for in prayer, believe that you have received it, and it will be yours.
25 And when you stand to pray, if you hold anything against someone, forgive it. Then your Father in heaven will forgive your wrongs too."
26 [Some manuscripts add: "But if you do not forgive, neither will your Father in heaven forgive your wrongs."]
27 They returned to Jerusalem. Jesus was walking in the temple area. The chief priests, scribes, and elders came up to him.
28 They asked, "By what authority are you doing these things? And who gave you the authority to do them?"
29 "I will ask you one question," Jesus replied. "If you answer me, I will tell you by what authority I am doing these things.
30 John's baptism — was it from heaven or from men? Answer me!"
31 They talked together about what to answer. "If we say, 'From heaven,' he will ask, 'Why didn't you believe him?'
32 But if we say, 'From men'…" — they were afraid of the people. Everyone believed that John truly was a prophet.
33 So they answered Jesus, "We don't know." And Jesus replied, "Then I won't tell you by what authority I am doing these things."

title: Mark 12
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Mark
paragraphs: 1 Then Jesus began to speak to them in parables. "A man planted a vineyard. He put a wall around it, dug a wine vat, and built a watchtower. Then he rented it out to some farmers and went on a long trip.
2 At harvest time, he sent a servant to the farmers to get his share of the fruit of the vineyard.
3 But they grabbed the servant, beat him, and sent him away empty-handed.
4 Then the owner sent them another servant. They hit him on the head and treated him badly.
5 He sent another, and this one they killed. He sent many more. Some they beat, and others they killed.
6 Finally, he had one son left — his beloved son. He sent him to them, saying, 'They will respect my son.'
7 But the farmers said to one another, 'This is the heir. Come, let us kill him, and the inheritance will be ours.'
8 So they grabbed him, killed him, and threw him out of the vineyard.
9 What then will the owner of the vineyard do? He will come and kill those farmers. He will give the vineyard to others.
10 Have you never read this Scripture? 'The stone the builders rejected has become the cornerstone.
11 This is from the Lord, and it is wonderful in our eyes.'"
12 At this, the leaders wanted to arrest Jesus. They knew he had told this parable against them. But they were afraid of the crowd, so they left him and went away.
13 Later, they sent some of the Pharisees and Herodians to trap Jesus in what he said.

14 "Teacher," they said, "we know that you are honest and don't care what people think. You treat everyone the same and teach God's way truthfully. Is it right to pay taxes to Caesar or not? Should we pay them or not?"
15 But Jesus saw through their pretending and said, "Why are you testing me? Bring me a Roman coin so I can look at it."
16 So they brought it. He asked them, "Whose picture is this? And whose name is on it?" "Caesar's," they answered.
17 Then Jesus told them, "Give to Caesar what is Caesar's, and to God what is God's." And they were amazed at him.
18 Then the Sadducees, who say there is no resurrection, came to Jesus and asked him:
19 "Teacher, Moses wrote for us that if a man's brother dies and leaves a wife but no children, the man must marry his brother's widow and have children for him.
20 Now there were seven brothers. The first one got married and died, leaving no children.
21 Then the second one married the widow, but he also died and left no children. The third did the same.
22 None of the seven left any children. And last of all, the woman died.
23 In the resurrection, then, whose wife will she be? All seven were married to her."
24 Jesus said to them, "Aren't you wrong because you don't know the Scriptures or the power of God?
25 When the dead rise, they will not marry, nor will they be given in marriage. They will be like the angels in heaven.
26 Now about the dead rising — have you not read in the Book of Moses about the burning bush? God said to Moses, 'I am the God of Abraham, the God of Isaac, and the God of Jacob.'
27 He is not the God of the dead, but of the living. You are very wrong!"
28 One of the scribes came up and heard their discussion. He saw how well Jesus had answered. So he asked Jesus, "Which commandment is the most important of all?"
29 Jesus replied, "This is the most important: 'Hear, O Israel, the Lord our God, the Lord is one.
30 Love the Lord your God with all your heart and with all your soul and with all your mind and with all your strength.'
31 The second is this: 'Love your neighbor as yourself.' There is no commandment greater than these."
32 "Right, Teacher," the scribe replied. "You have said it well. God is one, and there is no other but him.

33 To love him with all your heart and with all your understanding and with all your strength — and to love your neighbor as yourself — this is more important than all burnt offerings and sacrifices."
34 Jesus saw that the man had answered wisely. He said, "You are not far from the kingdom of God." And no one dared to ask him any more questions.
35 While Jesus was teaching in the temple area, he asked, "How can the scribes say that the Christ is the Son of David?
36 David himself, speaking by the Holy Spirit, said: 'The Lord said to my Lord, "Sit at my right hand until I put your enemies under your feet."'
37 David himself calls him 'Lord.' So how can he be David's son?" And the large crowd listened to him gladly.
38 In his teaching Jesus also said, "Watch out for the scribes. They love to walk around in long robes. They love to be greeted in the marketplaces.
39 They want the best seats in the synagogues and the best places at dinners.
40 They take houses from widows by trick. To show off, they make long prayers. These men will receive harsher punishment."
41 Jesus sat down across from the temple treasury. He watched the crowd putting money into it. Many rich people put in large amounts.
42 Then a poor widow came and put in two small copper coins — worth only a very small amount.
43 Jesus called his disciples to him and said, "I tell you the truth: this poor widow has put in more than all the others.
44 They all gave out of their extra money. But she, out of her poverty, has put in everything she had to live on."

title: Mark 13
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Mark
paragraphs: 1 As Jesus was leaving the temple, one of his disciples said to him, "Teacher, look at the huge stones and the magnificent buildings!"
2 "Do you see all these great buildings?" Jesus replied. "Not one stone here will be left on another. Every one of them will be torn down."
3 Jesus was sitting on the Mount of Olives, across from the temple. Peter, James, John, and Andrew came to him in private and asked,
4 "Tell us, when will these things happen? And what will be the sign that they are about to take place?"
5 Jesus began by telling them, "Be careful that no one fools you.
6 Many will come in my name, claiming, 'I am he,' and they will fool many.
7 When you hear of wars and reports of wars, do not be worried. These things must happen, but the end is still to come.
8 Nation will rise against nation, and kingdom against kingdom. There will be earthquakes in many places, and famines too. These are the beginning of birth pains.
9 Be careful. You will be handed over to the councils and beaten in the synagogues. Because of me, you will stand before governors and kings as witnesses to them.
10 And the gospel must first be preached to all the nations.
11 When they arrest you and hand you over, do not worry ahead of time about what to say. Just speak whatever you are given at that time. For it will not be you speaking, but the Holy Spirit.
12 Brother will betray brother to death, and a father his child. Children will rise up against their parents and have them put to death.
13 Everyone will hate you because of my name. But the one who keeps going to the end will be saved.
14 When you see 'the awful thing that destroys' standing where it should not be (let the reader understand), those in Judea must run to the mountains.
15 No one on the rooftop should go back inside to take anything from his house.
16 And no one in the field should return for his coat.
17 How sad those days will be for pregnant women and mothers feeding babies!
18 Pray that this will not happen in the winter.
19 For those days will be days of terrible suffering. There has never been such suffering from the beginning of God's creation until now. And there will never be such suffering again.
20 If the Lord had not cut those days short, no one would be saved. But for the sake of the chosen ones, whom he has chosen, he has cut those days short.
21 At that time, if anyone says to you, 'Look, here is the Christ!' or 'There he is!' do not believe it.
22 False Christs and false prophets will appear. They will do signs and miracles to fool even the chosen ones, if that were possible.
23 So be careful. I have told you everything ahead of time.
24 But in those days, after that terrible suffering: 'The sun will be darkened, and the moon will not give its light.
25 The stars will fall from the sky, and the powers in the sky will be shaken.'
26 At that time, they will see the Son of Man coming in the clouds with great power and glory.
27 He will send out the angels to gather his chosen ones from the four directions — from one end of the earth to the other end of heaven.
28 Now learn this lesson from the fig tree: as soon as its branches become soft and put out leaves, you know that summer is near.
29 In the same way, when you see these things happening, know that he is near — right at the door.
30 I tell you the truth: this generation will not pass away until all these things have happened.
31 Heaven and earth will pass away, but my words will never pass away.
32 No one knows about that day or hour — not even the angels in heaven, nor the Son, but only the Father.
33 Be careful and stay alert! You do not know when the set time will come.
34 It is like a man who goes on a long journey. He leaves his house. He puts each servant in charge of his own work. He tells the doorkeeper to keep watch.
35 So keep watch, because you do not know when the master of the house will return. It might be in the evening, at midnight, when the rooster crows, or in the morning.
36 Otherwise, he might come back suddenly and find you sleeping.
37 What I say to you, I say to everyone: Keep watch!"

title: Mark 14
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Mark
paragraphs: 1 The Passover and the Feast of Unleavened Bread were two days away. The chief priests and scribes were looking for a secret way to arrest Jesus and kill him.
2 "But not during the feast," they said, "or there may be a riot among the people."

3 Jesus was in Bethany at the home of Simon the Leper. While he was sitting at the table, a woman came in with an alabaster jar of expensive perfume, made of pure nard. She broke open the jar and poured the perfume on Jesus' head.
4 Some of those present were angry. They said to one another, "Why was this perfume wasted?
5 It could have been sold for more than 300 denarii, and the money given to the poor." And they scolded her.
6 But Jesus said, "Leave her alone. Why are you bothering her? She has done a beautiful thing to me.
7 The poor you will always have with you. You can help them whenever you want. But you will not always have me.
8 She has done what she could. She has anointed my body ahead of time for my burial.
9 I tell you the truth: wherever the gospel is preached in all the world, what she has done will also be told in memory of her."
10 Then Judas Iscariot, one of the Twelve, went to the chief priests to betray Jesus to them.
11 They were glad to hear this. They promised to give him money. So Judas began to look for a chance to betray Jesus.
12 It was the first day of the Feast of Unleavened Bread, when the Passover lamb was killed. Jesus' disciples asked him, "Where do you want us to prepare for you to eat the Passover?"
13 So he sent two of his disciples. He told them, "Go into the city. A man carrying a jug of water will meet you. Follow him.
14 Whichever house he enters, say to the owner, 'The Teacher asks: Where is my guest room, where I may eat the Passover with my disciples?'
15 He will show you a large upper room, with everything ready. Make our preparations there."
16 So the disciples left and went into the city. They found everything as Jesus had told them. And they prepared the Passover.
17 When evening came, Jesus arrived with the Twelve.
18 While they were sitting and eating, Jesus said, "I tell you the truth: one of you who is eating with me will betray me."
19 They became sad. They began to ask him one after another, "Surely it is not me?"
20 He answered, "It is one of the Twelve — the one who is dipping his hand into the bowl with me.
21 The Son of Man will go just as it is written about him. But that man who betrays him will have terrible sorrow. It would be better for him if he had never been born."
22 While they were eating, Jesus took bread. He said a blessing and broke it. He gave it to the disciples and said, "Take it. This is my body."
23 Then he took the cup, gave thanks, and gave it to them. They all drank from it.
24 He said to them, "This is my blood of the covenant, which is poured out for many.
25 I tell you the truth: I will not drink of the fruit of the vine again until the day when I drink it new in the kingdom of God."
26 When they had sung a hymn, they went out to the Mount of Olives.
27 Then Jesus said to them, "You will all fall away. For it is written: 'I will strike the Shepherd, and the sheep will be scattered.'
28 But after I have risen, I will go ahead of you into Galilee."
29 Peter said, "Even if all of them fall away, I will not."
30 "I tell you the truth," Jesus replied, "this very night, before the rooster crows twice, you will deny me three times."
31 But Peter kept saying, "Even if I have to die with you, I will never deny you." And all the others said the same.
32 Then they came to a place called Gethsemane. Jesus told his disciples, "Sit here while I pray."
33 He took Peter, James, and John with him. He began to be deeply troubled and full of sorrow.
34 He said to them, "My soul is full of sorrow, to the point of death. Stay here and keep watch."
35 Going a little farther, he fell to the ground. He prayed that, if it were possible, this hour might pass from him.
36 "Abba, Father," he said, "all things are possible for you. Take this cup from me. Yet not what I want, but what you want."
37 Then Jesus came back and found them sleeping. "Simon," he said, "are you asleep? Could you not keep watch for one hour?
38 Watch and pray, so that you will not fall into temptation. The spirit is willing, but the body is weak."
39 Again he went away and prayed, saying the same thing.
40 Again he came back and found them sleeping. Their eyes were heavy. They did not know what to say to him.
41 When Jesus came back the third time, he said, "Are you still sleeping and resting? That is enough. The hour has come. Look, the Son of Man is betrayed into the hands of sinners.
42 Get up, let us go. See — my betrayer is coming!"
43 While Jesus was still speaking, Judas, one of the Twelve, arrived. He came with a crowd armed with swords and clubs. They were sent from the chief priests, scribes, and elders.
44 The betrayer had given them a sign: "The one I kiss is the man. Arrest him and lead him away safely."
45 Going right up to Jesus, Judas said, "Rabbi!" and kissed him.
46 Then the men grabbed Jesus and arrested him.
47 One of the people standing nearby pulled out his sword. He struck the servant of the high priest and cut off his ear.
48 Jesus said to the crowd, "Have you come out with swords and clubs to arrest me, as if I were a rebel?
49 Every day I was with you, teaching in the temple area. You did not arrest me then. But this has happened so that the Scriptures would be fulfilled."
50 Then everyone deserted Jesus and ran away.
51 One young man who had been following Jesus was wearing a linen cloth around his body. They caught hold of him.
52 But he pulled free of the linen cloth and ran away naked.
53 They led Jesus away to the high priest. All the chief priests, elders, and scribes came together.
54 Peter followed Jesus at a distance, right into the courtyard of the high priest. He sat with the officers and warmed himself by the fire.
55 The chief priests and the whole Sanhedrin were looking for evidence against Jesus to put him to death. But they did not find any.
56 Many people gave false testimony against him, but their statements did not match.
57 Then some men stood up and gave false testimony against him:
58 "We heard him say, 'I will destroy this temple made by human hands. In three days I will build another, not made by human hands.'"
59 But even their testimony did not match.
60 So the high priest stood up before them and questioned Jesus, "Have you no answer? What is this that these men are saying against you?"
61 But Jesus stayed silent and said nothing. Again the high priest questioned him, "Are you the Christ, the Son of the Blessed One?"
62 "I am," said Jesus. "And you will see the Son of Man sitting at the right hand of the Mighty One, and coming with the clouds of heaven."
63 At this, the high priest tore his clothes. He said, "Why do we need any more witnesses?
64 You have heard the blasphemy. What is your verdict?" And they all said he deserved to die.
65 Then some of them began to spit on him. They blindfolded him, hit him with their fists, and said to him, "Prophesy!" And the officers received him with slaps in his face.
66 While Peter was in the courtyard below, one of the servant girls of the high priest came down.
67 She saw him warming himself there. She looked at Peter and said, "You were with Jesus the Nazarene too."
68 But Peter denied it. "I do not know what you are talking about," he said. "I don't even understand." Then he went out to the gateway. And the rooster crowed.
69 When the servant girl saw him there, she again said to those standing nearby, "This man is one of them."
70 But Peter denied it again. After a little while, those standing nearby said again to Peter, "You really are one of them. You are a Galilean too."
71 But he began to curse and swear, "I do not know this man you are talking about!"
72 Right then the rooster crowed a second time. Then Peter remembered what Jesus had said to him: "Before the rooster crows twice, you will deny me three times." And he broke down and cried.

title: Mark 15
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Mark
paragraphs: 1 Early in the morning, the chief priests, elders, scribes, and the whole Sanhedrin made a plan. They tied Jesus up, led him away, and handed him over to Pilate.
2 So Pilate questioned him, "Are you the King of the Jews?" "You have said so," Jesus replied.
3 The chief priests began to accuse him of many things.
4 Then Pilate questioned him again, "Have you no answer? Look how many charges they are bringing against you!"
5 But to Pilate's amazement, Jesus said nothing more.
6 Now it was Pilate's habit at the feast to release to the people a prisoner of their choice.
7 A man named Barabbas was in prison with the rebels. They had committed murder during an uprising.
8 So the crowd went up and began asking Pilate to keep his habit.
9 "Do you want me to release to you the King of the Jews?" Pilate asked.
10 He knew that the chief priests had handed Jesus over out of envy.
11 But the chief priests stirred up the crowd. They wanted him to release Barabbas to them instead.
12 So Pilate asked them again, "Then what do you want me to do with the one you call the King of the Jews?"
13 They shouted back, "Crucify him!"
14 "Why?" asked Pilate. "What evil has he done?" But they shouted all the louder, "Crucify him!"
15 Pilate wanted to please the crowd. So he released Barabbas to them. He had Jesus whipped, and handed him over to be crucified.
16 The soldiers led Jesus away into the palace (called the Praetorium). They called the whole group of soldiers together.
17 They dressed him in a purple robe. They twisted together a crown of thorns and set it on his head.
18 They began to salute him: "Hail, King of the Jews!"
19 They kept hitting his head with a stick and spitting on him. They knelt down and bowed before him.
20 After they had made fun of him, they took off the purple robe and put his own clothes back on him. Then they led him out to crucify him.
21 Simon of Cyrene was passing by on his way in from the country. He was the father of Alexander and Rufus. The soldiers forced him to carry the cross of Jesus.
22 They brought Jesus to a place called Golgotha, which means "The Place of the Skull."
23 They offered him wine mixed with myrrh, but he did not take it.
24 And they crucified him. They divided his clothes by throwing lots to decide what each of them would take.
25 It was the third hour when they crucified him.
26 The words written against him said: THE KING OF THE JEWS.
27 Along with Jesus, they crucified two robbers — one on his right and one on his left.
28 [Some manuscripts add: "And the Scripture was fulfilled which says, 'And he was numbered with the lawbreakers.'"]
29 Those who passed by hurled insults at him. They shook their heads and said, "Aha! You who are going to destroy the temple and rebuild it in three days,
30 come down from the cross and save yourself!"
31 In the same way, the chief priests and scribes made fun of him among themselves. They said, "He saved others, but he can't save himself!
32 Let the Christ, the King of Israel, come down now from the cross, so that we may see and believe!" And even those who were crucified with him insulted him.
33 From the sixth hour until the ninth hour, darkness came over the whole land.
34 At the ninth hour, Jesus cried out in a loud voice, "Eloi, Eloi, lema sabachthani?" — which means, "My God, my God, why have you forsaken me?"
35 When some of those standing nearby heard this, they said, "Look, he is calling Elijah."
36 Someone ran and filled a sponge with sour wine. He put it on a reed and held it up for Jesus to drink. He said, "Leave him alone. Let us see if Elijah comes to take him down."
37 But Jesus let out a loud cry and took his last breath.
38 The curtain of the temple was torn in two, from top to bottom.
39 The centurion was standing there in front of Jesus. When he saw how Jesus had died, he said, "Truly this man was the Son of God!"
40 There were also women watching from a distance. Among them were Mary Magdalene, Mary the mother of James the younger and of Joses, and Salome.
41 These women had followed Jesus and taken care of him while he was in Galilee. There were many other women who had come up to Jerusalem with him.
42 It was already evening. It was Preparation Day, the day before the Sabbath.
43 Joseph of Arimathea was an important Council member. He himself was waiting for the kingdom of God. He boldly went to Pilate to ask for the body of Jesus.
44 Pilate was surprised to hear that Jesus was already dead. So he called the centurion to ask if this was true.
45 When Pilate had confirmed it with the centurion, he gave the body to Joseph.
46 Joseph bought a linen cloth. He took down the body of Jesus and wrapped it in the cloth. He placed it in a tomb cut out of the rock. Then he rolled a stone against the entrance to the tomb.
47 Mary Magdalene and Mary the mother of Joseph saw where his body was placed.

title: Mark 16
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Mark
paragraphs: 1 When the Sabbath was over, Mary Magdalene, Mary the mother of James, and Salome bought spices. They wanted to go and anoint the body of Jesus.
2 Very early on the first day of the week, just after sunrise, they went to the tomb.
3 They were asking one another, "Who will roll away the stone from the entrance of the tomb?"
4 But when they looked up, they saw that the stone had been rolled away. It was extremely large.
5 When they went into the tomb, they saw a young man dressed in a white robe. He was sitting on the right side, and they were afraid.
6 But he said to them, "Do not be afraid. You are looking for Jesus the Nazarene, who was crucified. He has risen! He is not here! See the place where they put him.
7 But go, tell his disciples and Peter, 'He is going ahead of you into Galilee. There you will see him, just as he told you.'"

8 So the women left the tomb and ran away. They were shaking and confused. In their fear, they did not say a word to anyone. Note: The earliest manuscripts of Mark end at verse 8. Verses 9-20 appear in most later manuscripts and are included in most Bibles. Some Bibles mark them as a later addition.
9 Early on the first day of the week, after Jesus had risen, he appeared first to Mary Magdalene. He had driven seven demons out of her.
10 She went and told those who had been with him. They were mourning and crying.
11 When they heard that Jesus was alive and that she had seen him, they did not believe it.
12 After this, Jesus appeared in a different appearance to two of them as they were walking in the country.
13 They went back and told the rest. But they did not believe them either.
14 Later, as the Eleven were eating, Jesus appeared to them. He rebuked them for their unbelief and their hard hearts. They had not believed those who had seen him after he had risen.
15 He said to them, "Go into all the world and preach the gospel to every creature.
16 Whoever believes and is baptized will be saved. But whoever does not believe will be condemned.
17 These signs will follow those who believe: In my name they will drive out demons. They will speak in new tongues.
18 They will pick up snakes with their hands. If they drink any deadly poison, it will not hurt them. They will put their hands on the sick, and they will be made well."
19 After the Lord Jesus had spoken to them, he was taken up into heaven. He sat down at the right hand of God.
20 They went out and preached everywhere. The Lord worked with them, confirming his word by the signs that followed it.
